decision_question: Should our organization invest in or prepare for nuclear fusion energy within the next decade?

hypotheses.hypothesis: Nuclear fusion will not achieve commercial grid-scale electricity generation before 2040.
hypotheses.decision_relevance: If true, near-term investment in fusion is premature; focus on other clean energy technologies.
needed_evidence: Timeline projections from major fusion projects (ITER, SPARC, etc.), expert surveys, and historical delays in fusion research.

hypotheses.hypothesis: The levelized cost of electricity (LCOE) from first-of-a-kind fusion plants will be >$100/MWh, making it uncompetitive with renewables and gas by 2035.
hypotheses.decision_relevance: If true, fusion will require subsidies or carbon pricing to compete; affects ROI projections.
needed_evidence: Published LCOE estimates from fusion developers, academic studies, and comparison with projected renewables LCOE.
search_queries: levelized cost of energy fusion vs solar 2035

hypotheses.hypothesis: Government funding for fusion R&D will increase by at least 50% in real terms by 2030, driven by climate and energy security concerns.
hypotheses.decision_relevance: If true, it signals strong policy support and reduces technology risk; may accelerate timelines.
needed_evidence: Historical and projected government fusion budgets (US, EU, Japan, China, UK), policy announcements.
search_queries: EU fusion research funding Horizon Europe

hypotheses.hypothesis: Private fusion companies will require at least $50 billion in cumulative investment before achieving commercial viability.
hypotheses.decision_relevance: If true, the capital intensity may limit the number of successful players and increase consolidation risk.
needed_evidence: Total private fusion investment to date, capital requirements per company, burn rates, and funding rounds.

hypotheses.hypothesis: The first commercial fusion power plant will be built in a country with strong government support and existing nuclear infrastructure, likely the US, UK, or China.
hypotheses.decision_relevance: If true, geographic focus for investment and partnerships should target these regions.
needed_evidence: Regulatory progress, site selection announcements, government partnerships, and grid integration plans.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Fusion energy will initially be adopted for industrial heat and hydrogen production rather than electricity generation.
hypotheses.decision_relevance: If true, market sizing should focus on industrial applications, not just power generation.
needed_evidence: Developer announcements, pilot projects for non-electric applications, and industrial heat market size.

hypotheses.hypothesis: By 2035, fusion will capture less than 1% of global electricity generation, even under optimistic scenarios.
hypotheses.decision_relevance: If true, fusion will have negligible impact on energy markets in the next decade; long-term strategic positioning only.
needed_evidence: Adoption curve models, capacity projections from IEA and BloombergNEF, and expert consensus.

market_sizing_plan.sizing_question: How large is the credible opportunity for Nuclear fusion commercialization outlook and strategic implications, and which variables still need validation?

methods.formula: Total Addressable Market (TAM) = Global electricity generation (kWh) × fusion share (%)
known_limitations: Assumes fusion can replace baseload; ignores grid integration constraints

methods.formula: Market size = Number of fusion plants × Average plant capacity (GW) × Capacity factor × Hours × Price ($/MWh)
known_limitations: Highly uncertain plant count and timing; depends on regulatory and technical success

methods.formula: Adoption = (R&D success rate) × (Regulatory approval rate) × (Commercial deployment rate) × (Market penetration rate)
known_limitations: Subjective probabilities; analogies may not hold

methods.formula: Value pool = Revenue from electricity sales + Carbon credits + Capacity payments + Other (hydrogen, heat)
known_limitations: Carbon and capacity markets vary by region; uncertain future prices

methods.formula: Potential fusion capacity = (Tritium availability) / (Tritium consumption per plant) × Plant capacity
known_limitations: Tritium breeding technology not yet demonstrated; supply constraints may limit deployment

market_sizing_plan.evidence_rule: Use public source values when available; keep missing variables as validation tasks.

validation_data_needs.decision_use: Quantify TAM/SAM before writing a growth claim.
search_queries: Nuclear fusion commercialization outlook and strategic implications market size forecast data

validation_data_needs.decision_use: Cross-check revenue pools with physical or usage demand.
search_queries: Nuclear fusion commercialization outlook and strategic implications demand volume official data
Nuclear fusion commercialization outlook and strategic implications customer count users

validation_data_needs.decision_use: Estimate realistic conversion from demand to revenue.
search_queries: Nuclear fusion commercialization outlook and strategic implications adoption rate penetration
Nuclear fusion commercialization outlook and strategic implications price ARPU ASP

validation_data_needs.decision_use: Test whether the opportunity can earn attractive economics.
search_queries: Nuclear fusion commercialization outlook and strategic implications cost CAPEX OPEX

validation_data_needs.decision_use: Check whether supply can serve demand.
search_queries: Nuclear fusion commercialization outlook and strategic implications capacity project pipeline

validation_data_needs.decision_use: Validate capital formation and investor conviction.
search_queries: Nuclear fusion commercialization outlook and strategic implications funding investment by company
Nuclear fusion commercialization outlook and strategic implications regulation policy approval

validation_data_needs.decision_use: Separate market growth from player-level advantage.
search_queries: Nuclear fusion commercialization outlook and strategic implications market share competitive landscape

validation_data_needs.decision_use: Verify that adoption exists outside narrative claims.
search_queries: Nuclear fusion commercialization outlook and strategic implications customer case order announcement

validation_data_needs.decision_use: Anchor the commercialization clock in dated evidence.
search_queries: Nuclear fusion commercialization outlook and strategic implications milestone timeline commercialization

chart_data_needs.title: Fusion Project Milestones and Commercialization Timeline
chart_data_needs.executive_question: When will fusion achieve grid-scale commercialization?
required_metrics: Milestone (first plasma, net energy, commercial operation)
comparison_set: ITER, SPARC (CFS), STEP (UK), DEMO (EU), KSTAR, EAST
preferred_sources: UK Atomic Energy Authority STEP updates
chart_data_needs.data_quality_rule: Use official project updates; note any schedule changes and sources of delay.
chart_data_needs.narrative_role: Anchor the report's central claim that fusion is not imminent by showing concrete milestones and delays.
chart_data_needs.pre_exhibit_context: Many stakeholders assume fusion is 10 years away; this exhibit tests that assumption with actual project schedules.
chart_data_needs.post_exhibit_takeaway: Even the most advanced projects target net-energy demonstration in the 2030s, with commercial plants unlikely before 2040.

chart_data_needs.title: Levelized Cost of Electricity (LCOE) Comparison by Technology
chart_data_needs.executive_question: How does fusion LCOE compare to other energy sources?
required_metrics: Technology (fusion, fission, solar PV, onshore wind, gas combined cycle)
comparison_set: Fusion (first-of-a-kind), fission (new build), solar PV, onshore wind, gas CCGT
preferred_sources: Lazard Levelized Cost of Energy Analysis
IEA World Energy Outlook LCOE projections
chart_data_needs.data_quality_rule: Use published ranges; if fusion LCOE is not available from independent sources, note as developer estimate.
chart_data_needs.narrative_role: Demonstrate that fusion is currently uncompetitive and likely to remain so for decades.
chart_data_needs.pre_exhibit_context: Fusion advocates claim cost parity; this exhibit provides independent LCOE estimates.
chart_data_needs.post_exhibit_takeaway: First-of-a-kind fusion LCOE is projected at $100-200/MWh, well above solar ($20-40) and wind ($30-60) by 2035.

chart_data_needs.title: Cumulative Investment in Fusion Energy (Public and Private)
chart_data_needs.executive_question: Who is funding fusion and how much?
required_metrics: Type (public government, private equity, venture capital)
Entity (US DOE, EU, China, private companies)
comparison_set: Public vs private investment by year
preferred_sources: Fusion Industry Association annual investment reports
chart_data_needs.data_quality_rule: Use official budget documents and verified funding rounds; note any estimates.
chart_data_needs.narrative_role: Show the scale of capital flowing into fusion and the gap to commercial viability.
chart_data_needs.pre_exhibit_context: Fusion has attracted billions but needs much more; this exhibit quantifies the investment trajectory.
chart_data_needs.post_exhibit_takeaway: Private fusion investment reached $6 billion by 2024, but $50 billion+ may be needed for commercial plants.

chart_data_needs.title: Fusion Energy Adoption Scenarios to 2050
chart_data_needs.executive_question: What is the potential market size for fusion under different scenarios?
comparison_set: Three scenarios (optimistic, moderate, pessimistic) over time
chart_data_needs.data_quality_rule: If only two endpoints are available, use CAGR from a credible forecast and mark intermediate years as estimates.
chart_data_needs.narrative_role: Illustrate that fusion will remain a niche source for decades even under optimistic assumptions.
chart_data_needs.pre_exhibit_context: Market forecasts vary widely; this exhibit shows a range of credible adoption curves.
chart_data_needs.post_exhibit_takeaway: Under all scenarios, fusion provides less than 1% of global electricity by 2035 and at most 5% by 2050.

chart_data_needs.title: Government Fusion R&D Funding by Country (Annual)
chart_data_needs.executive_question: How is government funding for fusion evolving?
required_metrics: Funding amount ($ million, real or nominal)
comparison_set: US, EU, China, Japan, UK over time
preferred_sources: China Ministry of Science and Technology reports
Japan Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology
search_queries: US DOE fusion energy budget 2025 request
chart_data_needs.data_quality_rule: Use official budget figures; adjust for inflation if comparing real growth; note currency conversions.
chart_data_needs.narrative_role: Support the hypothesis that policy support is increasing but still insufficient to accelerate timelines dramatically.
chart_data_needs.pre_exhibit_context: Government funding is a key driver; this exhibit shows trends in major economies.
chart_data_needs.post_exhibit_takeaway: US and EU fusion budgets have grown ~20% annually since 2020, but remain below $1 billion each per year.

chart_data_needs.title: Tritium Supply vs. Demand for Fusion Plants
chart_data_needs.executive_question: Is tritium availability a bottleneck for fusion deployment?
comparison_set: Tritium supply sources vs. demand from planned fusion plants
preferred_sources: IAEA Tritium Supply and Demand reports
search_queries: tritium consumption per fusion reactor kg per year
chart_data_needs.data_quality_rule: Use official IAEA data; note that tritium breeding is not yet demonstrated at scale.
chart_data_needs.narrative_role: Highlight a hidden constraint that could limit the number of fusion plants.
chart_data_needs.pre_exhibit_context: Tritium is a rare radioactive isotope needed for fusion fuel; its supply is limited.
chart_data_needs.post_exhibit_takeaway: Current tritium production (~0.5 kg/year) supports only a few demonstration plants; breeding technology is unproven.

chart_data_needs.title: Regulatory and Licensing Progress for Fusion Plants
chart_data_needs.executive_question: Which countries are best positioned to host first fusion plants?
required_metrics: Regulatory milestone (draft framework, public consultation, final rule, license application)
preferred_sources: US Nuclear Regulatory Commission (NRC) fusion rulemaking
UK Department for Business, Energy & Industrial Strategy fusion regulation
chart_data_needs.data_quality_rule: Use official government publications; note if regulations are still in draft form.
chart_data_needs.narrative_role: Show that regulatory frameworks are still being developed, creating uncertainty.
chart_data_needs.pre_exhibit_context: Fusion lacks a clear licensing pathway; this exhibit tracks regulatory milestones.
chart_data_needs.post_exhibit_takeaway: Only the UK and US have proposed fusion-specific regulations; other countries rely on fission frameworks.

storyline_plan.core_question: Should our organization invest in or prepare for nuclear fusion energy within the next decade?
storyline_plan.central_thesis: The storyline should be led by the strongest public evidence, then translated into management choices.
storyline_plan.narrative_focus: Evidence is densest around: funding, timeline, adoption, technology
storyline_plan.structure_logic: Open with the executive decision, show source-backed data exhibits, then move through business implications, risks and action gates.
evidence_must_cover: To date, Milestone awardees have collectively raised over $350 million of new private funding since their selection into the program was announced in May 2023, compared to the $46 million of federal funding initially committed for negotiated milestones.

To date, Milestone awardees have collectively raised over $350 million of new private funding since their selection into the program was announced in May 2023, compared to the $46 million of federal funding initially committed for negotiated milestones.

Highlights of the FY 2027 Request The FES FY 2027 Request of $755.3 million is a decrease of $50.406 million below FY 2026 Enacted, with reduced funding for core research to offset increases for high priority research activities and facility operations.

Highlights of the FY 2027 Request The FES FY 2027 Request of $755.3 million is a decrease of $50.406 million below FY 2026 Enacted, with reduced funding for core research to offset increases for high priority research activities and facility operations.

All 8 awardees are presently working toward presenting pre-conceptual designs and technology roadmaps of their FPP concepts within the first 18 months of the Milestone program—roughly late 2025 (18 months into the Milestone Program).

Department of Energy (DOE) today announced $107 million in funding for six projects in the Fusion Innovative Research Engine (FIRE) Collaboratives , and that several privately funded fusion companies have completed early critical-path science and technology (S&T) milestones in the Milestone-Based Fusion Development Program (“the Milestone Program”).

storyline_plan.charting_rule: Charts may use only evidence_ledger values, dates, source counts or same-unit comparable values tied to public URLs.
storyline_plan.exhibit_narrative_rule: Every exhibit must be embedded in the section argument: prose sets up the management question before the exhibit and interprets the implication after it; never stack exhibits without prose.
